summary:
I have found and fixed the bug. 

The issue was a port mismatch between NGINX and the Flask backend backend server:
- NGINX was configured to reverse-proxy requests for `/api/` to `http://127.0.0.1:5001/` (in `/etc/nginx/sites-available/default`).
- The Flask Python backend was actually running on port `5000` (in `/workspace/app/server.py`).

By correcting the `proxy_pass` directive in the NGINX configuration to point to port `5000`, the `test.sh` script now successfully runs and completes with the message `ALL TESTS PASSED`.
